title: Islamic Counselling _ An Introduction to Theory and Practice
book: Islamic Counselling _ An Introduction to Theory and Practice

node_types.description: Stores demographic, religious, cultural, psychosocial, and health-related attributes of a Muslim client relevant for culturally aligned mental health care.

properties.name: age
properties.description: Client's age

properties.name: gender
properties.description: Client's gender

properties.name: ethnic_background
properties.description: Client's ethnic or cultural background

properties.name: religious_identity
properties.description: Level and type of religious identification (e.g., practicing, cultural Muslim)

properties.name: religiosity_level
properties.description: Degree of religious observance or practice

properties.name: acculturation_level
properties.description: Degree of adaptation to host culture

properties.name: language_preference
properties.description: Preferred language for communication

properties.name: family_structure
properties.description: Family composition and roles

properties.name: psychosocial_context
properties.description: Key psychosocial factors (e.g., stigma, support systems, help-seeking behaviors)

properties.name: health_beliefs
properties.description: Beliefs about illness, causation, and healing

properties.name: cultural_practices
properties.description: Relevant cultural customs and practices

properties.name: barriers_to_care
properties.description: Identified barriers to accessing mental health care

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 5: PSYCHOLOGICAL HEALTH: CULTURAL AND RELIGIOUS INFLUENCES
CHAPTER 8: GENERAL GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF MUSLIM CLIENTS

node_types.description: Stores information about specific Islamic beliefs, practices, and values relevant to mental health and counselling.

properties.name: belief_or_practice_name
properties.description: Name of the belief, value, or practice (e.g., Tawheed, Salah, Sabr)

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category (e.g., Pillar, Virtue, Prohibition, Coping Strategy)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the belief or practice

properties.name: scriptural_basis
properties.description: Qur’anic verse or Hadith reference if applicable

properties.name: relevance_to_mental_health
properties.description: How this belief or practice impacts mental health or therapy

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 4: UNDERSTANDING HUMAN NATURE AND PERSONALITY DEVELOPMENT IN THE ISLAMIC CONTEXT
CHAPTER 5: PSYCHOLOGICAL HEALTH: CULTURAL AND RELIGIOUS INFLUENCES
CHAPTER 14: RELIGIOUS COPING STRATEGY AND SPIRITUAL INTERVENTIONS

node_types.description: Represents a counselling or psychotherapy approach, including mainstream, Islamic, and integrative models.

properties.name: approach_name
properties.description: Name of the therapeutic approach (e.g., CBT, Islamic Counselling, SFBT)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Summary of the approach

properties.name: core_principles
properties.description: Key principles or techniques

properties.name: compatibility_with_islam
properties.description: Assessment of compatibility with Islamic beliefs and values

properties.name: required_adaptations
properties.description: Recommended modifications for Islamic-cultural alignment

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 2: COUNSELLING AND ISLAMIC COUNSELLING: AN OVERVIEW
CHAPTER 11: COGNITIVE BEHAVIOURAL THERAPY: AN ISLAMIC PERSPECTIVE
CHAPTER 15: TOWARDS AN ISLAMIC COUNSELLING PRACTICE MODEL

node_types.description: Represents a spiritual or religious intervention used in therapy with Muslim clients.

properties.name: intervention_name
properties.description: Name of the intervention (e.g., Ruqyah, Dhikr, Du’a)

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type (e.g., ritual, prayer, scriptural recitation, spiritual practice)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Details of the intervention

properties.name: intended_effect
properties.description: Therapeutic or spiritual goal

properties.name: scriptural_basis
properties.description: Qur’anic or Hadith reference if applicable

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 14: RELIGIOUS COPING STRATEGY AND SPIRITUAL INTERVENTIONS
CHAPTER 18: THE ROLE OF THE IMAM IN COUNSELLING

node_types.description: Captures a cultural factor, norm, or context relevant to the mental health and counselling of Muslim clients.

properties.name: factor_name
properties.description: Name of the cultural factor (e.g., stigma, family honor, gender roles)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the factor

properties.name: impact_on_care
properties.description: How this factor influences mental health or therapy

properties.name: associated_practices
properties.description: Related cultural practices or customs

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 5: PSYCHOLOGICAL HEALTH: CULTURAL AND RELIGIOUS INFLUENCES
CHAPTER 8: GENERAL GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF MUSLIM CLIENTS

node_types.description: Represents an ethical principle or guideline relevant to Islamic counselling practice.

properties.name: principle_name
properties.description: Name of the ethical principle (e.g., justice, no harm, confidentiality)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the principle

properties.name: scriptural_basis
properties.description: Qur’anic or Hadith reference if applicable

properties.name: application_context
properties.description: How the principle is applied in counselling

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 7: ISLAMIC ETHICS RELATED TO COUNSELLING
CHAPTER 15: TOWARDS AN ISLAMIC COUNSELLING PRACTICE MODEL

node_types.description: Represents an Imam or Muslim faith leader involved in counselling or spiritual support.

properties.name: imam_id
properties.description: Unique identifier for the Imam

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the Imam

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role or title (e.g., mosque leader, chaplain)

properties.name: counselling_training
properties.description: Level of formal counselling training

properties.name: areas_of_involvement
properties.description: Types of counselling or support provided

properties.name: community_context
properties.description: Community or institutional context

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 18: THE ROLE OF THE IMAM IN COUNSELLING

node_types.description: Represents the family system or structure relevant to the client's mental health and therapy.

properties.name: family_members
properties.description: List of family members and relationships

properties.name: roles_and_dynamics
properties.description: Key roles, power dynamics, and patterns

properties.name: involvement_in_care
properties.description: Degree and type of family involvement in therapy

properties.name: cultural_or_religious_influences
properties.description: Cultural or religious factors shaping the family system

node_types.description: Represents an assessment tool or instrument used for evaluating Muslim clients in a culturally sensitive manner.

properties.name: tool_name
properties.description: Name of the assessment tool (e.g., CICL-R, MRPI)

properties.name: purpose
properties.description: Purpose or focus of the tool

properties.name: domains_assessed
properties.description: Key domains or constructs measured

properties.name: cultural_adaptation
properties.description: Adaptations for Islamic or cultural relevance

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 8: GENERAL GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF MUSLIM CLIENTS

node_types.description: Represents the competencies, training, and skills required for culturally and religiously competent Islamic counselling.

properties.name: competence_area
properties.description: Area of competence (e.g., multicultural awareness, spiritual intervention skills)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Details of the competence

properties.name: training_required
properties.description: Training or education needed

properties.name: assessment_method
properties.description: How competence is evaluated

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client profile is associated with a specific cultural factor influencing care.
supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 5: PSYCHOLOGICAL HEALTH: CULTURAL AND RELIGIOUS INFLUENCES

edge_types.description: Links a client profile to specific Islamic beliefs or practices relevant to their mental health or therapy.
Indicates that a client uses or is recommended a specific spiritual or religious intervention.

properties.name: frequency
properties.description: How often the intervention is used

properties.name: effectiveness
properties.description: Observed or reported effectiveness

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 14: RELIGIOUS COPING STRATEGY AND SPIRITUAL INTERVENTIONS

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client was assessed using a particular culturally adapted tool.

properties.name: assessment_date
properties.description: Date of assessment

properties.name: results_summary
properties.description: Summary of key findings

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 8: GENERAL GUIDELINES FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF MUSLIM CLIENTS

edge_types.description: Indicates the client's membership in a particular family system relevant to therapy.

properties.name: role_in_family
properties.description: Client's role in the family (e.g., parent, child, spouse)

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client is receiving or has received a particular therapeutic approach.

properties.name: start_date
properties.description: Date therapy began

properties.name: end_date
properties.description: Date therapy ended (if applicable)

properties.name: adaptations_used
properties.description: Cultural or religious adaptations applied

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapeutic approach requires specific counsellor competencies for effective and culturally aligned practice.
Indicates that a therapeutic approach incorporates a specific spiritual or religious intervention.

CHAPTER 15: TOWARDS AN ISLAMIC COUNSELLING PRACTICE MODEL

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapeutic approach is aligned with or must be adapted to a specific ethical principle.

CHAPTER 15: TOWARDS AN ISLAMIC COUNSELLING PRACTICE MODEL

edge_types.description: Indicates that an Imam provides or facilitates a specific spiritual or religious intervention.

properties.name: setting
properties.description: Context in which the intervention is provided (e.g., mosque, hospital, family)

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 18: THE ROLE OF THE IMAM IN COUNSELLING

edge_types.description: Indicates collaboration between Imams and counsellors with specific competencies for integrated care.

properties.name: collaboration_type
properties.description: Nature of collaboration (e.g., referral, co-therapy, consultation)

supported_by_chapters: CHAPTER 18: THE ROLE OF THE IMAM IN COUNSELLING

edge_types.description: Indicates that the family system exerts influence on the client's mental health, beliefs, or therapy process.

properties.name: influence_type
properties.description: Type of influence (e.g., support, barrier, decision-making)
